steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The first sentence explains the technique: smothering the flame around the vessel. The second sentence states what happens to the vessel: it becomes shiny and black. The smothering causes the color change.

steps.title: Pick the transition that signals cause-and-effect
steps.content: "As a result" introduces an effect that follows from a stated cause — exactly the relationship between smothering the flame and the resulting black hue.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'On the contrary,': signals contradiction, but the second sentence does not contradict the first; it states the outcome. ✗
  • Choice B — 'For example,': signals an instance of a general claim, but the black hue is not an example of smothering the flame — it is its consequence. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Previously,': signals a prior point in time, but the black hue happens after, not before, the smothering. ✗
  • Choice D — 'As a result,': correctly marks the black hue as the effect of smothering the flame. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
